channel.title: Potatoes on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Potatoes on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Tomato Soup Potatoes
item.description:
Submitted by Hazel Pigeau

Grandmother of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 lb hamburg
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • 5 or 6 medium potatoes, sliced
  • 1 can Heinz tomato soup

Instructions

Brown hamburg and onions. Place potatoes in a greased casserole dish. Add hamburg and onions. Mix in tomato soup.

Cook at 350 degrees for 1 hour, or until potatoes are tender.

item.title: Campbell's Broccoli Chicken Potato Parmesan
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 8 mini red-skinned potatoes, thinly sliced
  • 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves, cut in strips
  • 1 can Cream of Broccoli Soup
  • ½ cup milk
  • ¼ tsp garlic powder
  • 2 cups fresh or frozen broccoli florets
  • 1/3 cup grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add potato slices and stir fry until tender crisp.

Add chicken and continue to stir fry until chicken is browned on all sides. Reduce heat to medium.

item.title: Potato "Playdough"
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 or 5 potatoes
  • 1 to 1-1/2 cups flour

Instructions

Bake 4 or 5 potatoes in the skin until soft inside. Peel and discard the peel. Mash potatoes (Use a potato ricer to get a nice fine even texture. Combine 1 to 1-1/2 cups flour to every 2 cups of mashed potato and form a dough.

The author’s son likes to play with this dough just so he can eat the scraps. It has no raw egg in it and leaving it out for a bit, there is nothing in it to spoil. The color will gray after a while, but you add food coloring. When you make it, you won’t even notice. If you have extra, roll it into balls and press your thumb in one side and roll it across the back side of a fork. Cook in boiling water for 1 minute and serve either with tomato sauce or butter or even brown gravy.

item.title: Tarragon Potato Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/3 lbs red potatoes, unpeeled
  • 1 small clove garlic
  • ¾ tsp dried tarragon
  • ½ cup no-oil Italian dressing
  • ½ tsp dried parsley flakes
  • 2 Tbsp minced green onions
  • ½ tsp dry mustard
  • ¾ tsp lemon juice

Instructions

Cook potatoes in boiling water to cover 15 minutes or until tender. Drain well, and cool. Cut into ¼” slices, and set aside. Combine tarragon, parsley, mustard and garlic in container of an electric blender. Cover and process 30 seconds or until pureed. Add Italian dressing and process until blended. With blender running, slowly add lemon juice, and continue to process dressing mixture until smooth. Add dressing mixture to cooked potato slices; add minced green onions, and toss well to coat. Cover and chill. Yield: 8(1/2 cup) servings.

item.title: Creamed Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 medium round red potatoes, peeled and sliced
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 small onion, peeled and sliced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 4 green onions, coarsely chopped
  • 1 cup skim milk
  • 2 (20 oz) cans no-salt added chicken broth, undiluted
  • 1/8 tsp ground white pepper
  • 1/8 tsp ground nutmeg

Instructions

Combine first 5 ingredients in a 3-quart saucepan; cover and simmer 20 minutes or until potato is tender. Transfer potato mixture in batches to container of an electric blender or food processor; cover and process until smooth. Combine pureed mixture with milk and remaining ingredients. Serve warm or chilled Yield: 9 (3/4 cup) servings.

item.title: Fluffy Potato Casserole
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Cups of mashed potatoes, hot or cold
  • 1 250g package of cream cheese, softened
  • 1 small onion, finely chopped
  • 3 Tbsp of flour
  • Salt & Pepper to taste

Instructions

Add everything together.  Beat until light and fluffy.  Spoon into greased casserole dish.  Bake at 300 degrees for 35 mins (maybe more).

item.title: Loaded "Baked Potato" Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Medium baking potatoes, unpeeled
  • 1 can (10 fl oz.) chicken broth
  • 1 cup milk
  • 3 slices of cooked bacon (can be the ready-to-serve microwave stuff)
  • 3/4 cup of shredded cheddar cheese, divided
  • 2 Tbsp sliced green onions, divided
  • 1/4 cup sour cream

Instructions

  1. Pierce potatoes; microwave on HIGH 5 mins., turning over after 2 1/2 mins.  Cut potatoes in chunks.  Combine potatoes, broth, and milk in large microwaveable bowl.  Microwave on HIGH 6 mins., stirring after 3 min.  Slightly crush potatoes with a potato masher.
  2. Reserve 2 Tbsp. each of the bacon and cheese and 1 Tbsp. of the onions for topping; set aside.   Add remaining bacon, cheese, and onions to soup.
  3. Serve topped with reserved bacon, cheese, onions, and sour cream.

item.title: Potato Soup (Outback Steakhouse)
item.description:
Outback Steakhouse Potato Soup

Ingredients

  • Potatoes (4 large or up to 8 small potatoes)
  • Kosher Salt
  • Vegetable Oil
  • 2 sticks of butter
  • 1 Onion, diced
  • 3/4 cup of all purpose flour
  • 48 oz of chicken broth
  • 2 cups water
  • Celery salt to taste
  • 1/2 tsp ground black pepper
  • 1/4 tsp dried basil
  • 1/4 tsp sugar
  • 1 1/2 cups heavy cream
  • 4-6 slices of cooked bacon, chopped
  • Chopped chives (depends on your preference)
  • 2 cups shredded Monterrey Jack / Cheddar cheese blend.

Method

Preheat your oven to 350 degrees

item.title: Crispy Garlic Baked Potato Wedges
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 large russet potatoes, rinsed
  • 1/4 cup olive oil
  • 1 tablespoon minced garlic, (or 3 teaspoons garlic powder)
  • 1/2 teaspoon onion powder
  • 2 teaspoons salt, (adjust to your tastes)
  • 1 teaspoon paprika
  • 1/2 teaspoon black cracked pepper
  • 2/3 cup finely grated or shredded parmesan cheese, divided
  • 2 tablespoons fresh chopped parsley

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 390°F (200°C). Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper; set aside.
  2. Cut each potato in half lengthways, then cut each half in half lengthways again, and then cut each half in half lengthways again until you have 8 wedges. (Make sure they are about the same thickness and size).
  3. In a small jug, combine the oil, garlic, onion powder, salt, paprika annd pepper. Pour the oil mixture over the potatoes and toss together to coat evenly.
  4. Arrange potato wedges in a single layer on a large baking tray, skin-sides-down. Sprinkle with half of the parmesan cheese and extra salt if needed.
  5. Bake for 35 minutes, turning wedges half way through baking time, until golden, crisp and cooked through when tested with a fork.
  6. Sprinkle with parsley and remaining parmesan cheese to serve.

Billy’s Notes: These were great! Some of the best home-made potato things like this I think I’ve tried.

item.title: Garlic and Rosemary Mashed Potatoes
item.description:
Ingredients

Ingredients

  • 5-6 Large Potatoes Peeled and cubed You can use more or less with the same cooking time
  • 1 cup chicken broth
  • 3 cloves garlic
  • 1 sprig rosemary
  • 2 tbs butter
  • 1/4 cup milk

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Place cubed potatoes in the Instant pot with chicken broth whole cloves of garlic, rosemary sprig, and butter.
  2. Place IP on manual high pressure for 15 minutes.
  3. Do a quick release.
  4. Drain Potatoes if needed and add milk and butter
  5. Mash until creamy and smooth. I use my kitchen aid mixer or a handheld masher.

Here is a link to the actual website. Credit Adventures of a Nurse:

item.title: Sam's Roasted Red Pepper & Potato Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 red peppers
  • Half a red onion
  • 4 cloves of garlic
  • 2 medium sized potatoes (yellow or white)
  • A carton of vegetable broth
  • Salt & Pepper (to taste)
  • Cayenne pepper (to taste)
  • A gob of butter

Instructions:

Instructions

  1. Turn on your oven broiler to high.
  2. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray with some cooking spray.
  3. Cut each pepper into fours and line on the baking sheet.
  4. Put in oven and let broil until skins start to turn black.
  5. Once skins have turned black, take out of oven and put into a large mixing bowl.  Place plate on top of it to trap the heat in.  This helps the skin soften so they are easy to remove.  Let the peppers stay in the bowl for 10 minutes.
  6. While you wait, mince your onion and garlic.
  7. Wash and peel potatoes and cut them into cubes.
  8. Once 10 minutes have passed, carefully peel the skins off the peppers.
  9. Once the skin has been peeled off, chop up the peppers.
  10. In a large soup pot, melt butter at the bottom over medium heat.  Combine your onion and garlic.  Sauté for about 2 minutes, until the onions become soft.
  11. Add potatoes and combine for about another minute.
  12. Add in red peppers and stir everything together for about a minute.
  13. Season with salt and pepper.
  14. Empty carton of vegetable broth into the pot, stirring occasionally.
  15. Let soup simmer on low to medium heat, until potatoes are soft.
  16. Carefully sample the broth and add cayenne pepper if needed for an extra bit of spice.
  17. Carefully remove from stovetop and use immersion blender to bend sopu to a velvety texture.

item.title: Air Fryer Mashed Potato Balls
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups mashed potatoes (preferably made with butter and cream)
  • 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 cup panko breadcrumbs
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder (optional)
  • 1/2 teaspoon onion powder (optional)
  • Olive oil spray (or any cooking spray)

Instructions

  1. Prepare the Mashed Potatoes:If you haven’t already, prepare mashed potatoes. Ensure they are smooth and slightly on the thicker side (not too runny).
  2. Mix the Ingredients:In a large bowl, combine the mashed potatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder (if using). Stir until the cheese is evenly distributed.
  3. Form the Balls:Scoop about 1 tablespoon of the mashed potato mixture and roll it into a ball. Continue until all the mixture is used. You should be able to form 12–16 balls, depending on their size.
  4. Coat the Balls:Set up a breading station:In one shallow dish, place the flour.In another dish, beat the egg.In a third dish, add the panko breadcrumbs.Roll each mashed potato ball first in the flour, then dip in the egg, and finally coat it in the panko breadcrumbs. Press gently to ensure the breadcrumbs stick well.
  5. Preheat the Air Fryer:Preheat your air fryer to 375°F (190°C) for about 3-5 minutes.
  6. Air Fry the Mashed Potato Balls:Lightly spray the air fryer basket with olive oil.Place the potato balls in the air fryer basket, leaving some space between them for air circulation.Lightly spray the tops of the balls with more olive oil.Air fry at 375°F for 8-10 minutes or until they are golden brown and crispy, flipping them halfway through the cooking time for even crispiness.
  7. Serve:Once golden and crispy, remove the mashed potato balls from the air fryer. Let them cool slightly before serving.You can serve these with a dipping sauce, such as sour cream, ranch, or ketchup!

Tips:
